codeReview.summary: The feature largely meets the UX goals: shared sectioned forms are implemented for both new/edit flows, help text and responsive grouping are present, defaults are applied, and API shape remains intact. I found two non-blocking correctness/behavior gaps in edge cases.

issues.description: When Estimated Duration is set to "Custom", the free-text input is stored only in local state (`customDuration`) and is never propagated back to `formData.estimatedDuration` (see lines 630, 646-647). Both submit handlers send only `formData.estimatedDuration` (`src/app/business/projects/new/page.tsx:108`, `src/app/business/projects/[id]/edit/page.tsx:163`), so custom text is lost and "Custom" is submitted instead.

Edit mode defaults missing compensation type to `paid-hourly` (`projectData.compensationType || 'paid-hourly'`). For legacy records lacking a stored value, opening and saving can silently convert unspecified/experience compensation to hourly, which may misstate compensation intent.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `fullstack-worker` skill procedure requires lint as part of mandatory automated verification, but this worker run did not include lint while `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` was marked true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/fullstack-worker/SKILL.md (Step 4 includes `npm run lint`); handoff verification.commandsRun lists only `npm run type-check`, `npm run build`, and a curl check.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission AGENTS says "No `any` types", but new feature code introduced explicit `as any` casts, suggesting convention enforcement/clarity gaps in shared state.

summary: Reviewed feature ux-opportunity-form for commit d3bb8ff using handoff, diff, transcript skeleton, and skill/convention docs. Implementation satisfies the requested UX redesign and preserves API flow, with two non-blocking edge-case issues (custom duration persistence and edit fallback compensation default) plus shared-state observations around skill-procedure/validation alignment and convention enforcement.
